name: codex-array-command-deny
doc: Regression for the fail-open where Codex sends `tool_input.command` as an argv ARRAY (`["bash","-lc","<script>"]`) instead of a string. normalize.ts previously coerced the array with a `typeof === string` check → `undefined`, so every `ctx.command` guard (bash-write, destructive-git) saw nothing and allowed. commandToString (runtime/command-string.ts) unwraps `[shell, -lc|-c, script]` to the script so the ARRAY payload reaches the SAME deny as the STRING form in scenario 19. Verified 2026-07-07.

steps.doc: Heredoc redirected into a .ts file, wrapped as a Codex argv array → commandToString returns the script → bashWriteGuard blockCodeWrite deny. Same block as scenario 19 step 1, only the wire shape differs (array not string).

`git push --force` as an argv array matches GIT_BLOCKED → deny "Destructive git command" (evaluate.ts:22-34). Pre-fix the array collapsed to command=undefined and this force-push sailed through — the proven fail-open.

`git commit` as an argv array matches GIT_ASK; Codex has no interactive approval so the ask is downgraded to an explicit deny (codex/index.ts toCodexResponse). This is the exact `git commit` that fail-open let through live.
A plain read (`ls`) wrapped as an argv array: no guard, no git match → allow (empty stdout). Confirms the array unwrap does not fabricate false denies on safe commands.
